zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugo Fitzwarin Lee於1142年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾時，他的父親Fulk FitzWarin I27歲，母親Eva de Whittington and Alveston34歲。他和Christian de Stainton至少有1個兒子。他在英國史洛普爾被列為3rd sheriff of shropshire。他去世於1180年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾皮爾享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugo Fitzwarin Lee于1142年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾时，他的父亲Fulk FitzWarin I27岁，母亲Eva de Whittington and Alveston34岁。他和Christian de Stainton至少有1个儿子。他在英国史洛普爾被列为3rd sheriff of shropshire。他去世于1180年在英国英格兰史洛普爾皮爾享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Hugo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Hugh  , used throughout the Middle Ages in official documents, and now enjoying a revival as a given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugo Fitzwarin Lee was born in 1142, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Fulk FitzWarin I, was 27 and his mother, Eva de Whittington and Alveston, was 34. He had at least 1 son with Christian de Stainton. His occupation is listed as 3rd sheriff of shropshire in Shropshire, England. He died in 1180, in Pimhill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugo Fitzwarin Lee est né en 1142, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Fulk FitzWarin I, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Eva de Whittington and Alveston, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Christian de Stainton. Sa profession de son indiquée est 3rd sheriff of shropshire à Shropshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé en 1180, à Pimhill, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugo Fitzwarin Lee 1142 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fulk FitzWarin I 27 und seine Mutter Eva de Whittington and Alveston 34. Er hatte mit Christian de Stainton mindestens 1 Sohn. sein Beruf ist als 3rd sheriff of shropshire in Shropshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb 1180 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Pimhill, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo Fitzwarin Lee è nato il nel 1142 nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Fulk FitzWarin I, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Eva de Whittington and Alveston, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Christian de Stainton. La sua occupazione è indicata come 3rd sheriff of shropshire nel Shropshire, England. Morì nel 1180, nel Pimhill, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 38 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugo Fitzwarin Leeが1142年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Fulk FitzWarin Iは27歳，母Eva de Whittington and Alvestonは34歳であった。彼はChristian de Staintonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。イギリスシュロプシュアイアでの職業は3rd sheriff of shropshireと記録されている。彼は1180年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアピムヒルで38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugo Fitzwarin Lee(이)가 1142년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fulk FitzWarin I(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Eva de Whittington and Alveston(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Christian de Stainton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 슈럽실르에서 3rd sheriff of shropshire이었다. 그는 1180년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 피힐에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugo Fitzwarin Lee nasceu em 1142, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Fulk FitzWarin I, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Eva de Whittington and Alveston, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Christian de Stainton. Sua profissão aparece como 3rd sheriff of shropshire em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 1180, em Pimhill, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 38 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugo Fitzwarin Lee родился в 1142 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Fulk FitzWarin I было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Eva de Whittington and Alveston, 34. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Christian de Stainton.  Он работал по профессии 3rd sheriff of shropshire в местности шропшир, Англия. Он умер в 1180 г. в местности пимхилл, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugo Fitzwarin Lee nació en 1142, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Fulk FitzWarin I, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Eva de Whittington and Alveston, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Christian de Stainton. Su ocupación registrada era 3rd sheriff of shropshire en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Murió en 1180, en Pimhill, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 38 años.
